behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert very specific implementation details not mentioned in instruction.md: test_tab_checks_element_via_query_selector checks for literal 'page.$(selector)' or 'this.page.$(selector)' strings; test_tab_error_message_quoted_format checks for exact string '"${param.selector}" does not match'; test_tab_locator_parser_integration checks for a regex pattern 'import\s*\{[^}]*locatorOrSelectorAsSelector' and requires /tmp/locatorParser.cjs to exist. The instruction mentions page.$() but doesn't

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states to use 'locatorParser.ts' utilities, use 'page.$()' for element existence checking, use the exact error format '"${param.selector}" does not match any elements.', and specifies the function 'requires the language identifier, the raw selector string, and the testIdAttribute configuration value as arguments'. This is essentially a step-by-step implementation guide, not just a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: test_tab_checks_element_via_query_selector requires exact string 'page.$(selector)' — an alternative using a different variable name like 'page.$(resolvedSelector)' would fail. test_tab_imports_locator_parser requires the exact function name 'locatorOrSelectorAsSelector'. test_tab_error_message_quoted_format checks for exact template literal format. A correct alternative using different variable names or a wrapper function would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The majority of tests (test_tab_imports_locator_parser, test_tab_converts_selector_through_parser, test_tab_checks_element_via_query_selector, test_tab_error_message_quoted_format, test_skill_md_*) are purely text-matching via 'assert X in content'. Only test_tab_locator_parser_integration and test_repo_locator_parser_unit execute code, but they test the pre-existing parser module, not the agent's changes. test_repo_eslint and test_repo_build run subprocesses but are pass-to-pass regression test

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for specific strings like 'locatorOrSelectorAsSelector', 'page.$(selector)', and quoted error format that don't exist in the base commit. A stub or no-op implementation wouldn't add these strings to the files, so the tests would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four behaviors specified in the instruction (import/use locator parser, use page.$(), quoted error message, update SKILL.md) are covered by corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into sections (fail-to-pass code behavioral, fail-to-pass documentation, pass-to-pass static, pass-to-pass repo tests) with clear comments and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task doesn't produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, and variable names in instruction.md are consistent and correct with the actual repository structure.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact files to modify (tab.ts, SKILL.md), the exact utility module to use (locatorParser.ts), the exact function signature pattern (language, raw selector, testIdAttribute), the exact method to use (page.$()), and the exact error message format. This goes well beyond describing the bug.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold diff for SKILL.md is localized to the 'Targeting elements' section, replacing a few lines. The tab.ts diff is similarly localized to one code block. Neither contradicts other files.
